summary:
The patient experienced joint pain and fever before developing a rash and hyperpigmentation after using NSAIDs; this suggests a fixed drug eruption due to recurrent exposure to an irritant similar to these medications. Summary:

A fixed drug eruption was diagnosed based on the onset of skin manifestations including rash and hyperpigmentation shortly after use of NSAIDs, consistent with reactions from previous exposures. Key Points: Joint pain/fever precede appearance of rash/hyperpigmentation triggered by NSAID intake indicative of fixed drug eruption. \(\blacksquare\)
